active_projects.description: Prove shift-closure of canonical FMCS families (CanonicalOmega is ShiftClosed) and bridge the sorry-free BFMCS truth lemma to standard semantics. Two theorems: (1) FMCS shift-closure — fam_shift(t) := fam(t + Δ) is an FMCS, trivial by translation invariance; (2) BFMCS-to-standard bridge — construct WorldHistories from FMCS families and show bmcs_truth_at ↔ truth_at, connecting existing BFMCS completeness to semantic_consequence. Depends on canonical task relation from task 956.

artifacts.summary: Deep analysis of shift-closure role in completeness: definition, why it is needed for MF/TF axiom soundness, proof strategy (trivial by translation invariance), BFMCS bypass and re-entry, WorldHistory construction from FMCS families

Bridge theorem necessity analysis: bmcs_truth_at already bypassed by CanonicalConstruction.lean; ShiftClosed Omega mathematically required; port shifted_truth_lemma pattern from Boneyard/IntRepresentation/Representation.lean
5-phase implementation plan: FMCS shift infrastructure, ShiftClosedCanonicalOmega, box_persistent, shifted_truth_lemma, completeness theorems
Implementation complete: ported shift-closure pattern from Boneyard to CanonicalConstruction.lean. Added ShiftClosedCanonicalOmega, box_persistent, shifted_truth_lemma. All sorry-free.

active_projects.description: Construct D as the group of order-preserving automorphisms (translations) of the canonical timeline of MCSs, with a designated origin. D emerges from pure syntax via: consistent set → MCS origin (Lindenbaum) → linear order T of reachable MCSs (existence lemma) → D = Aut+(T) (translation group) → eval₀ : D ≅ T (origin bijection) → linearly ordered abelian group → task_rel = group action → canonical model → truth lemma → completeness. Supersedes DovetailingChain, SuccOrder, and hardcoded D=Int approaches.

active_projects.description: Refactor unilateral proof system (Γ ⊢ φ) to bilateral system with dual judgments: acceptance (Γ ⊢⁺ φ) and rejection (Γ ⊢⁻ φ). Parallel implementation with equivalence bridge.

artifacts.summary: Detailed bilateral system design: BilateralDeriv type with acceptance/rejection rules, equivalence bridge to unilateral system, impact analysis on all downstream modules. Recommends parallel implementation preserving existing system.
Dependency analysis confirms tasks 953/954/955 are independent; 953 should proceed first due to zero dependencies and lower risk while 954/955 are blocked on task 951
Analysis of Demo.lean state, API survey, recommended updates (remove obsolete refs, add BFMCS truth lemma showcase, D construction via Cantor, shift-closure), implementation plan (4 phases, ~2 hours)
MCS-membership box semantics is mathematically sound; not a cheat. Two truth predicates differ structurally but yield equivalent completeness results via soundness chain.
Removal plan for bmcs_valid_mcs: 3 defs + 11 theorems to delete from ChainBundleBFMCS.lean lines 350-691. All _mcs code self-contained with zero external dependents. Keep CanonicalBC construction.
Deep analysis of canonical frame and world history definitions for bridging BFMCS and standard semantics
Deep analysis of how to model times D: identifies structural mismatch between CanonicalBC (Preorder) and standard validity (LinearOrderedAddCommGroup); recommends BFMCS soundness as bridge path
Parametric D strategy: Int is correct choice. Chain B (Representation.lean) already uses D=Int. Sole blocker is 2 sorries in DovetailingChain.lean for forward_F/backward_P witnesses.

Root cause: constant witness families cannot have temporal coherence. CanonicalBC works because F-witness MCS IS a new time point. Recommended: weaken temporal coherence to eval-family only, then tackle DovetailingChain sorries via omega-squared construction.
OBSOLETE: target definitions (bmcs_truth_at_mcs, bmcs_valid_mcs, bmcs_weak_completeness_mcs) in Boneyard; StagedConstruction is publication path. Recommend abandon Task 930.
